title: androsace halleri subsp. nuria
intro: Androsace halleri subsp. nuria, also known as Nuria's rock jasmine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Primulaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Androsace halleri subsp. nuria include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
